checks.a_analysis: I reviewed the output JSON against the source PDF. All paragraphs and sentences in the body of the resolution match the source document. The document title in metadata is an acceptable AI addition. Table headers like 'Field' and 'Value' and the signature transcriptions ('Mark F. Richardson', 'Susan O. Washburn') are based on visible printed text in the source and are not hallucinations.
The source document does not contain any contact information (phone numbers, emails, URLs). The output JSON correctly reflects this by not including any fabricated contact data.
The single-page source document's contents are entirely accounted for in the output JSON, including the header, title, preamble (whereas clauses), resolution, date, and signature details.

The signature block has been converted into a table. The source contains two distinct signature lines (Chairman and Clerk). The output table contains rows corresponding to both signees, effectively representing the source structure.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The main text of the resolution is preserved in full. No significant portions of the document (defined as >25% of a section) are missing.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: A comparison of the text shows that the word 'ATTEST:', which appears in the source PDF directly above the Clerk's signature line, is missing from the output JSON. This is a minor omission of a label found in the original document.
checks.b_evidence_output: [Text is absent from the signature area in the output]
checks.b_evidence_location: Signature block, above Susan O. Washburn's name, page 1, before element index 10

checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed the output against the source PDF. Each paragraph and heading from the source appears exactly once in the output. The signature information is structured into a table, which results in the names appearing in both the 'Field' and 'Value' columns; however, this is a standard extraction format for signature blocks and does not constitute a duplication of a text block from the source.

The output contains a single heading (level 1) which correctly represents the main title of the resolution. The text preceding it is correctly identified as a paragraph. No levels are skipped, and the hierarchy is logical for a single-page document.

The source document does not contain formal tables. The signature blocks at the end are represented in the output as a key-value table. This is an appropriate structural transformation for organizing signature data for accessibility. The table itself is well-formed with a header row and data rows corresponding to the two signatories.

The reading order in the output follows the logical top-to-bottom flow of the source document: header info, title, preambles (WHEREAS), resolution (NOW, THEREFORE), date, signatures, and finally the seal. While the 'ATTEST:' label is omitted, the sequence of the main sections is preserved correctly.
The source document does not contain any lists. The 'WHEREAS' clauses are correctly identified as individual paragraphs, which is appropriate for their structure in the resolution.
There are no links in the source document, and none have been erroneously added to the output.

checks.a_analysis: The visual organization of the HTML document faithfully represents the source PDF. The hierarchical structure of headings and the flow of text clauses are maintained. The conversion of the signature block into a table format is an appropriate method for presenting this information in an accessible web format and does not disrupt the visual flow or logic of the document.

The signature table ('Board of Commissioners Signatures') in the HTML is rendered correctly. It features clear, bordered cells, distinct headers, and properly aligned text. The table is fully legible and provides a clear representation of the signature fields from the original document.

All meaningful visual elements from the source PDF, specifically the Rockingham County seal and the handwritten signatures, are present in the HTML output. They are visible within the embedded image of the document located at the bottom of the page. No broken image icons or missing meaningful images were detected.

The body content of the resolution is visually duplicated in the HTML output. The text starting from 'NORTH CAROLINA ROCKINGHAM COUNTY' down to the date 'This 6th day of March, 2023' is first rendered as standard HTML text. Below this section and the signature table, the same content is repeated as a full-page image of the original document. This results in the complete text of the resolution appearing twice within the main body of the page.
checks.b_evidence_source: The entire text of the resolution on Page 1, beginning with 'NORTH CAROLINA ROCKINGHAM COUNTY' and ending with 'This 6th day of March, 2023.'
checks.b_evidence_output: The same resolution text is rendered once as standard text at the top of the document and then appears a second time inside the document image located at the bottom of the page.
